title: Fix/db fallback login ux
body: ## fix: improve login UX when cloud DB is unavailable ### Problem When the Neon cloud database exceeds its transfer quota, the backend silently falls back to local Postgres. Users trying to log in saw either a completely unhelpful "An error occurred" message, or no message at all — with no explanation of why their credentials weren't working. Three separate issues were found and fixed: 1. **Wrong error message** — The Axios error extractor only handled `{ error: { message: "..." } }` and `{ message: "..." }` response shapes. The auth endpoint returns `{ "error": "" }`, which fell through to the hardcoded `'An error occurred'` fallback. 2. **No context for the user** — The login page had no way to know the app was running against a local DB fallback, so it couldn't warn the user that their cloud account wouldn't be found. 3. **Race condition on redirect** — When a stored JWT fails (app bounces user back to `/login?returnUrl=...`), the async health check hadn't resolved yet before the user attempted to log in again, so the warning banner wasn't visible. ### Changes **Backend** - `app/__init__.py` — sets `app.config['DB_MODE'] = 'local_fallback'` when Neon is unreachable and the fallback fires - `app/controllers/routes.py` — health endpoint now includes `"db_mode": "cloud"` or `"db_mode": "local_fallback"` in its response **Frontend** - `src/services/api.ts` — fixed error message extraction to handle `{ "error": "" }` shape; auth failures now show the real backend message - `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` — three improvements: - Fetches `/api/health` on mount; shows yellow warning banner when `db_mode === 'local_fallback'` - Initializes error state immediately (synchronously) when `?returnUrl=` is present, eliminating the race condition - Moved `rawReturnUrl` parsing before state declarations so it can be used as initial state ### Result - Direct visit to `/login`: yellow warning banner appears as soon as health check resolves (~100ms) - Redirected back to `/login` after session failure: "Your session could not be verified" message appears instantly on render, yellow banner follows once health check resolves - Failed login attempt: shows real backend message "Invalid email or password." instead of "An error occurred" --- ## Summary by cubic Improves login UX when the cloud DB is unavailable by detecting local DB fallback and showing a clear banner. Also surfaces real auth errors and removes a redirect race. - **New Features** - Backend always sets `DB_MODE` to `'cloud'` or `'local_fallback'`; health endpoint returns `db_mode`. - Login page fetches health on mount and shows a warning banner on local fallback with neutral copy. - **Bug Fixes** - Error extraction now handles `{ error: string }` responses, so real auth messages show. - Shows an immediate “session could not be verified” message on redirect, removing the banner race. Written for commit 643bf4d03e3545e5653553af08ddd095573f6a63. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Login page displays a warning when the cloud database is unavailable. * **Improvements** * Better error message extraction from API responses. * Enhanced redirect URL handling on login page for improved navigation. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/18?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: fix: eliminate excessive HTTP polling and oversized list serialization
body: ## fix: Eliminate excessive HTTP polling and oversized list serialization ### Problem The app was exceeding Neon's monthly data transfer quota due to five distinct patterns of unnecessary HTTP traffic: 1. **Unconditional pipeline status polling** — `PipelineStatusContext` polled `/api/hubspot/pipeline/status` every 8 seconds regardless of whether a pipeline was running (~3,600 req/day per open tab at idle) 2. **Duplicate pipeline status polling** — `HubSpotImportArea` registered its own independent `useQuery` for the same endpoint, doubling the request rate when the import page was open 3. **Duplicate queue counts polling** — `App.tsx` registered its own `useQuery` for `['queue-counts']` alongside `QueueSidebar`, doubling that request rate 4. **Unconditional background polling** — All 7 queue page components and `QueueSidebar` polled their endpoints even when the browser tab was hidden 5. **Oversized list serialization** — `_serialize_property_summary` included `notes` (free text) and `mailer_history` (JSON) in every row of every list response, even though the list UI never displays those fields ### Fix **Frontend — conditional polling:** - `PipelineStatusContext`: `refetchInterval` changed from fixed `8000` to a function returning `false` when `pipeline_running` is `false`, `8000` when running - `HubSpotImportArea`: removed duplicate `useQuery` for pipeline status; now consumes `usePipelineStatus()` from context - `App.tsx`: removed duplicate `useQuery` for `['queue-counts']`; replaced with passive `queryClient.getQueryData()` cache read (no polling) - `QueueSidebar`: interval increased from 60s → 5 minutes (`5 * 60_000`); added `refetchIntervalInBackground: false` - All 7 queue page components: added `refetchIntervalInBackground: false` (polling pauses when tab is hidden; resumes on focus) - `WebhookSyncPanel`: both webhook queries now use conditional `refetchInterval` — 30s when `processed_count > 0`, 5 minutes when idle; added `refetchIntervalInBackground: false` to both **Backend — list serializer:** - `_serialize_property_summary`: removed `notes` and `mailer_history` keys - `_serialize_property_detail`: unchanged — both fields remain accessible on the detail page ### Preserved behavior All user-visible polling behavior is unchanged: - AppBar spinner and pipeline status chips update in near-real-time when a pipeline is running (≤ 8s interval) - `HubSpotImportArea` continues to poll `/api/hubspot/runs` at 5s when an import is active (existing conditional pattern untouched) - Queue tables refresh every 60s while the user is actively viewing them - Webhook log and summary refresh at 30s when there is recent activity - Badge counts in the sidebar remain current (5-minute background refresh) - Queue action invalidations (Log Call, Log Note, Create Task, Suppress, Reactivate) still trigger immediate refetch - Property list page displays all visible columns unchanged; only the two hidden fields are removed from the payload - `_serialize_property_detail` continues to include `notes` and `mailer_history` for the detail view ### Testing Added two property-based test suites following the exploratory bugfix methodology: **Bug condition tests** (`PollingBugCondition.test.tsx`, `test_polling_bug_condition.py`): - Written on unfixed code — all failed, confirming the bugs existed - Re-run on fixed code — all pass, confirming the bugs are resolved - Covers all 5 patterns (A–E) using static source analysis + Hypothesis PBT **Preservation tests** (`PollingPreservation.test.tsx`, `test_polling_preservation.py`): - Written on unfixed code — all passed, establishing the behavioral baseline - Re-run on fixed code — all pass, confirming no regressions - Covers requirements 3.1–3.9 (active polling, single-owner semantics, action invalidations, detail serializer) Updated `test_api_contract.py` to assert `mailer_history` is absent from list responses (correct post-fix contract). **Final checkpoint:** 501 frontend tests + 1486 backend tests — all passing. TypeScript build clean. --- ## Summary by cubic Cuts unnecessary HTTP polling and trims list payloads to reduce Neon data transfer without changing visible refresh behavior. Polling is now conditional, de-duplicated, and paused in background tabs; list responses exclude unused large fields. - **Bug Fixes** - Pipeline status: `PipelineStatusContext` polls only when running (8s); removed duplicate poll from `HubSpotImportArea`. - Queue counts: single owner in `QueueSidebar` (5-minute interval); `App.tsx` subscribes to the shared `['queue-counts']` cache with `staleTime: Infinity` (no polling); background polling disabled. - Queue pages: added `refetchIntervalInBackground: false` to all 7 queue components (still 60s when visible). - Webhooks: `WebhookSyncPanel` polls at 30s when `processed_count > 0`, otherwise 5 minutes; background polling disabled. - Backend lists: removed `notes` and `mailer_history` from `_serialize_property_summary`; detail endpoint unchanged. - Tests: added bug-condition and preservation suites; updated API contract to assert `notes` and `mailer_history` are absent from list responses. Written for commit 307b0f0badfdad0657502f9c95ca88afa0ff10b0. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Bug Fixes** * Eliminated excessive and duplicate API polling to improve performance and reduce server load. * Disabled periodic polling for inactive UI views and background tabs to save battery. * Removed legacy fields from list API responses to shrink payloads. * **Improvements** * Conditional polling: faster updates when active, longer/background intervals when idle. * Consolidated polling to shared sources to avoid duplicate requests. * **Tests & Docs** * Added validation tests and design/tasks docs to ensure behavior and preservation. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/17?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: Feature/om pdf intake fix
body: # feat: OM intake fixes, sale comps suggested workflow, dashboard improvements, and CI hardening ## Summary This PR covers a full session of bug fixes, feature improvements, and reliability hardening across the OM intake pipeline, multifamily sale comps workflow, dashboard, pro forma, and CI infrastructure. --- ## Bug Fixes - **App blank on load** — `AuthProvider` was missing from `main.tsx`; `LoginPage` component was never created. App showed a permanent spinner. - **Celery worker crash on startup** — `admin_service.py` imported `NotFoundError`/`ValidationError` which don't exist; `LoginSchema` missing from `schemas.py`; broken `down_revision` in migration `t0u1v2w3x4y5`. - **OM intake jobs stuck at PENDING** — Celery worker wasn't running; pdf_bytes deferred column syntax was wrong. - **Dashboard valuation sections always blank** — valuation was read from cached pro forma (always null); now computed live from confirmed sale comps. - **Dashboard cash flow showing wrong value** — was showing net CF (before debt service); now shows CF after debt service per scenario. - **Price-to-rent ratio wrong formula** — was `purchase_price / annualized_GSR`; now `monthly_rent / purchase_price`. - **Sale comp clear/confirm-all 404** — backend restart required to register new routes. --- ## OM Intake - `pdf_bytes` column marked as `deferred` — excluded from all standard SELECT queries - PDF bytes no longer stored in DB at all — passed directly as base64 Celery task argument - Eliminates the root cause of 26 GB/day Neon network transfer (pdf_bytes fetched on every 3-second status poll) --- ## Sale Comps — Suggested Workflow - AI-fetched comps saved as `is_suggested=True` pending user review - Out-of-range comps flagged with ⚠ badge instead of silently discarded - **Suggested Comps panel** with ✓ Add and ✗ Dismiss buttons per row - **Approve All** button confirms all pending suggestions at once - **Clear All** button hard-deletes all comps with confirmation dialog - Deduplication across fetches (dismissed comps excluded from dupe check) - Tiered search: pass 1 (2mi/12mo), pass 2 (5mi/24mo) if < 5 comps returned - Switched to `gemini-3.5-flash` model for better instruction following - Prompt rewritten to match real estate comp research format - Migration: adds `is_suggested`, `is_dismissed`, `out_of_range` columns to `sale_comps` --- ## Dashboard & Pro Forma - Dashboard valuation sections now populate from confirmed sale comps - Cash flow section shows CF after debt service per scenario - 4 new cash flow rows: CF/unit month 1, CF/unit month 24, CF needed for $200/door, purchase price for $200/door CF - Pro forma table shows full column names instead of acronyms, horizontally scrollable - "All values are monthly amounts" label above pro forma table --- ## Lender Profiles & Funding Sources - Seeded 4 default lender profiles (1 Construction-to-Perm + 3 Self-Funded Reno: FNBC, Barrington Bank, Albany Park Bank) - New deals auto-seeded with default funding sources (Cash $280k, HELOC1 $232k @9.375%, HELOC2 $300k @9.375%) --- ## CI & Reliability - GitHub Actions CI workflow: frontend (typecheck + tests + build) + backend (pytest) - Celery worker import smoke test in pre-commit hook and CI — catches broken imports before push - App shell smoke test catches blank-app regressions automatically - Exception aliases (`NotFoundError`, `ValidationError`) prevent future Celery crashes from name mismatches - Automatic DB fallback — app falls back to local PostgreSQL when Neon is unreachable (quota exceeded, network error) --- ## Testing - All pre-commit checks passed: Celery import OK, TypeScript clean, production build clean - Frontend test suite: 463 passing (5 pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR) --- ## Summary by cubic Speeds up OM PDF intake and comparable search by offloading to Celery with a safe synchronous fallback, and adds a suggested sale comps review flow. Tightens auth to header-only, improves dashboard/pro forma, stabilizes CI/tests, and adds `owner_user_id` to leads. - **New Features** - Comparable search: always enqueued via Celery (202) with a synchronous fallback; shared polling helper; switched to `gemini-3.5-flash`. - Sale comps: suggested-review flow (Add/Dismiss, Approve All/Clear All), dedupe, out‑of‑range flag; migration adds `is_suggested`, `is_dismissed`, `out_of_range`. - Dashboard/Pro Forma: valuation from confirmed comps; cash flow after debt; new per‑unit and target CF metrics; clearer labels and horizontal scroll. - App shell/Auth: `AuthProvider` wired; `LoginPage` added; identity must come from the `X-User-Id` header; new deals auto‑seed default funding sources; leads gain `owner_user_id`. - CI/Tests: GitHub Actions workflow; app shell smoke test; API failover/rate‑limit helpers; add `@rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu`, `bcrypt`, `PyJWT`; Hypothesis deadlines disabled globally. - **Bug Fixes** - OM intake: pipeline body moved to `app/services/om_intake/om_intake_pipeline.py` (no worker side effects); PDFs passed directly to Celery; `pdf_bytes` is deferred; synchronous fallback calls the pipeline module (fixed task name); no orphaned PENDING jobs; sync test runner no longer pushes a new app context. - Workflow/Comparable: atomic guard against duplicate step‑2 submissions; async step advancement fixed; safe numeric coercion; deduplicates pin‑less sales across radii; bbox queries use rounded coords. - HubSpot/Leads: deterministic/idempotent matching; unmatched contacts create a Contact + placeholder Lead; owner search checks concatenated names across Lead and Contact; timeline hides soft‑deleted entries. - Valuation/Health: rollup key casing fixed; classic price‑to‑rent ratio; health check supports degraded status. - Reliability/CI/Tests: Celery import and app shell smoke tests; global Hypothesis deadline suppression; webhook enqueue patch path corrected; 468 tests passing. Written for commit 6efef6af371db37d7edb4300a46445c880e07d59. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Login page + exported auth provider; AI-suggested sale comps UI with confirm/dismiss, confirm-all, and clear-all actions; endpoints to manage suggestions. * **Improvements** * Dashboard adds cash-flow-per-unit and purchase-price-for-target metrics; async polling for long-running AI tasks; longer AI task timeouts; safer DB startup fallback behavior. * **Bug Fixes** * Prevent duplicate comp insertion; PDF intake requires re-upload for failed jobs (avoids storing raw PDF bytes). * **Tests / CI** * App smoke and updated component tests; new CI workflow. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/16?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: feat: add admin panel with cross-user visibility
body: ## Admin Panel — Cross-User Visibility for Admins Adds a read-only admin panel that gives Ben full visibility across all platform users, their activity counts, and their leads. Non-admin users (User X) see no admin UI and are redirected away from admin routes. --- ## What's New ### Database - Alembic migration `t0u1v2w3x4y5` adds `is_admin BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` to the `users` table - Seeds `is_admin = TRUE` for `ben.d.staples.7@gmail.com` on migration run - New `User` SQLAlchemy model ### Backend - `AuthService` — JWT issuance with `is_admin` claim; `require_auth` sets `g.is_admin` from token - `require_admin` decorator — returns HTTP 403 with `{"error": "Forbidden", "message": "Admin access required."}` for non-admin users; logs attempt at WARNING level - `AdminService` — three read-only methods: `list_users`, `get_user_summary`, `list_leads` (with pagination and optional owner filter) - `admin_controller` Blueprint registered at `/api/admin` with three endpoints: - `GET /api/admin/users` — all users ordered by `created_at` asc, no credential fields - `GET /api/admin/users//summary` — profile + lead/list/job counts - `GET /api/admin/leads` — paginated cross-user lead list with optional `owner_user_id` filter ### Frontend - `AuthContext` — decodes `is_admin` from JWT on login and page load; rejects tokens with non-boolean `is_admin` as malformed; fixes base64url padding bug in JWT decoder - `AdminPanel` — fetches all users + summaries in parallel, MUI table with 8 columns, error/loading states, row click navigates to user detail - `AdminUserDetail` — user profile card + paginated leads table, back button to `/admin` - `App.tsx` — guarded `/admin` and `/admin/users/:userId` routes (non-admins redirected to `/`); "Admin Panel" menu item in avatar dropdown (Ben only); profile photo shown for Ben, initials avatar for other users ### Tests - 19 unit tests for `admin_controller` endpoints - 9 Hypothesis property-based tests (Properties 1, 3–9): JWT round-trip, route guards, user list ordering/credentials, summary counts, lead visibility/filtering/pagination - 15 `AuthContext` tests including Properties 2, 11, 17 (fast-check) - 10 `AdminPanel` component tests including Property 10 (route access control) - 3 `AdminUserDetail` component tests --- ## Testing - Log in as Ben → avatar menu shows "Admin Panel" → `/admin` shows user table - Log in as User X → no "Admin Panel" in menu → navigating to `/admin` redirects to `/` - `cd backend && pytest tests/test_admin_properties.py tests/test_admin_controller.py` — 28 passed - `cd frontend && npm test -- --run "AdminPanel|AdminUserDetail|AuthContext"` — 28 passed ## Migration Run `flask db upgrade head` in the `backend/` directory before starting the server. --- ## Summary by cubic Adds a read-only admin panel with cross-user visibility for admins using JWTs with an `is_admin` claim. Non-admins are blocked from `/api/admin` and `/admin`; auth is tightened (login rate limiting, case-insensitive Bearer handling), and admin leads pagination is deterministic. - **New Features** - Database: add `is_admin BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` to `users`; seed admin for `ben.d.staples.7@gmail.com` and fail the migration if that user is missing. - Auth: JWT issuance/verification with `is_admin`; `require_auth` populates `g.user_id`/`g.is_admin`; `require_admin` returns 403 for non-admins; enforce non-default `SECRET_KEY` outside tests; rate-limit `POST /api/auth/login` to 10/min; accept case-insensitive `Bearer` and reject non-`Bearer` `Authorization` headers; gate legacy `X-User-Id` via `ALLOW_LEGACY_X_USER_ID`. - Admin API (`/api/admin`): `GET /users`, `GET /users/:user_id/summary`, `GET /leads` (paginated with bounds checks, optional `owner_user_id` filter, and stable sorting with secondary `id DESC` tie-breaker). All read-only; no credential fields returned. - Frontend: `AuthContext` decodes `is_admin`, validates tokens (expiry window, finite `exp`/`iat`), uses UTF-8-safe JWT decoding, and guards `/admin` routes; new `AdminPanel` (user list + summaries) and `AdminUserDetail` (profile + paginated leads) with keyboard navigation and safe date formatting; fixes login-only layout to hide the app shell when unauthenticated. - API client: sends `Authorization: Bearer `; validates JWT before storing; clears session and redirects on 401 (skips `/auth/login`). - Fixes: base64url padding bug in the client JWT decoder; align bcrypt cost to reduce timing leaks; input validation on admin leads pagination. - **Migration** - Run: `cd backend && flask db upgrade head`. - Set a strong `SECRET_KEY` in `backend/.env` before starting the server. Written for commit 2f40c9616d74861d76cef16822b462ca65bf76b5. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** - Added user authentication system with login functionality - Introduced Admin Panel page for viewing all users and their activity summaries - Added admin user detail pages displaying individual user information and leads - Implemented admin-only access controls and permission enforcement - Added JWT-based session management for secure user authentication - Updated navigation to display admin menu for authorized users [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/15?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: feat: cloud database migration — startup guards, test suite, Neon mig…
body: ## feat: Cloud Database Migration — Startup Guards, Test Suite & Neon Migration ### Summary Migrates the B&B Real Estate Analysis Platform from a local PostgreSQL instance to a hosted Neon cloud database. Adds four startup validation guards to `create_app()` that enforce security, observability, and connection pool correctness on every server start. Includes a 22-test property-based and example-based test suite covering all 9 correctness properties defined in the spec. ### What Changed #### `backend/app/__init__.py` - **Guard 1 — `_validate_and_log_database_url()`**: Validates `DATABASE_URL` scheme (`postgresql`/`postgres` only), logs the resolved host with credentials redacted at INFO level, aborts startup with `SystemExit(1)` if missing or invalid. Skips in testing mode. - **Guard 2 — `_assert_pool_pre_ping()`**: Raises `RuntimeError` at startup if `pool_pre_ping=True` is absent from `SQLALCHEMY_ENGINE_OPTIONS` in non-testing configs. - **Guard 3 — `_assert_not_superuser()`**: Queries `pg_user` and aborts startup if the connected database user has superuser privileges on a remote (non-localhost) database. - **Guard 4 — `_warn_provider_dashboard()`**: Logs a startup WARNING with the provider's performance dashboard URL (from `PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URL` env var) or instructs the operator to set it. - Moved `setup_logging()` before the guards so all startup messages appear in the terminal console. - Set `app.config['TESTING'] = True` early in `create_app('testing')` so guards self-skip correctly in test mode. #### `backend/.env.example` - Added commented cloud-format `DATABASE_URL` placeholder with `sslmode=require` - Added commented `PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URL` placeholder - No real credentials, hostnames, or database names #### `backend/tests/test_cloud_database_migration.py` (new) 22 tests covering: - **Properties 1–9** (Hypothesis property-based): DATABASE_URL passthrough, fallback, connection pool invariant, superuser rejection, invalid URL abort, credential redaction, Alembic heads error, migration idempotency convention, connection pool budget - **Example-based**: Guard 1 edge cases (valid URL, postgres:// scheme, missing URL, mysql:// scheme), Guard 2 edge cases (testing skip, pool_pre_ping present/absent), Guard 4 (dashboard URL set/absent), `.env.example` and `.gitignore` correctness #### `.kiro/specs/cloud-database-migration/` - `requirements.md`, `design.md`, `tasks.md` — full spec documentation ### Data Migration - Exported 6,790 leads, 122 analysis sessions, and all schema objects from local PostgreSQL - Restored to Neon (us-east-1) with verified row count match - Alembic at head revision `r8s9t0u1v2w3` on cloud DB - `backend/.env` updated locally to point at Neon (not committed — gitignored) ### Testing - All 22 new tests pass - Zero regressions introduced (49 pre-existing failures unchanged) - Pre-commit hook: TypeScript check ✅, frontend build ✅ ### To switch between local and cloud DB In `backend/.env`, swap the `DATABASE_URL` line: ```dotenv # Cloud (Neon) DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...@ep-fancy-darkness-apn9dvhn.c-7.us-east-1.aws.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require # Local fallback # DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/real_estate_analysis ``` --- ## Summary by cubic Moves the backend to a Neon-hosted PostgreSQL and adds four startup guards for safer, clearer boot. Updates `.env.example`, enforces the superuser check in production, and expands the suite to 23 tests; logging now initializes before guards so messages are visible. - **New Features** - Startup guards: validate/log redacted `DATABASE_URL`, enforce `pool_pre_ping`, reject superuser on remote DBs, and warn with `PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URL`. - `.env.example` shows a cloud `DATABASE_URL` with `sslmode=require` and a `PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URL` placeholder. - **Bug Fixes** - Superuser guard now runs in all non-testing, non-Celery environments (production too) and still skips localhost. - Accept both `postgresql://` and `postgres://`; `_validate_and_log_database_url` uses exception chaining for clearer errors. - Tests: add a boundary check for the connection pool budget; restrict superuser tests to remote hosts; fix `migrations.md` path and assert presence; read `pool_size` from runtime config; relax Hypothesis deadlines. Total tests: 23. - Security: ignore `*.dump` and `*.sql.gz` in `.gitignore` to avoid committing database exports. Written for commit 54fb9f9e0bfc96fd9b46852841cbae79711e6be3. title: feat: HubSpot webhook sync — real-time deal/contact/company updates +…
body: ## HubSpot Webhook Sync — Real-Time CRM Updates ### Summary Adds ongoing one-way synchronization from HubSpot to the platform via webhooks. After the initial historical migration, this keeps deals, contacts, and companies current in real time as changes happen in HubSpot — without manual re-imports. Engagements (notes, calls, tasks) sync on an hourly schedule since HubSpot legacy apps don't support engagement webhook subscriptions. ### What's New **Backend** - `POST /api/hubspot/webhook` — unauthenticated endpoint with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification (HubSpot v3: millisecond timestamps, base64-encoded signatures, full URL in message) - Database migration adding `hubspot_webhook_logs`, `hubspot_sync_runs`, `hubspot_platform_writes` tables and `encrypted_client_secret` column on `hubspot_config` - `HubSpotWebhookService` — signature verification, batch ingestion, 24-hour log summary, retry dispatch - Celery processing pipeline: `process_event` → `fetch_and_upsert` → `incremental_matching` → `convert_activity` → `extract_signals` → `rescore_lead` - Deduplication (60s window) and loop guard (30s window, always-false until write-back enabled) - Webhook log API: `GET /webhook-log`, `GET /webhook-log/summary`, `POST /webhook-log/{id}/retry` - Config endpoints extended: `POST /config` accepts `client_secret`, `GET /config` returns `has_client_secret` - `encrypt_client_secret` / `decrypt_client_secret` static methods on `HubSpotClientService` - Auto-configure client secret from `HUBSPOT_CLIENT_SECRET` env var at startup - `hubspot.scheduled_engagement_sync` Celery beat task — hourly engagement import + full pipeline (configurable via `HUBSPOT_ENGAGEMENT_SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES`) **Frontend** - `WebhookSyncPanel` component — client secret input with "Configured ✓" badge, copyable webhook URL, setup instructions, stale sync warning, 24-hour summary counts, paginated webhook log table with Retry button - Integrated into `HubSpotImportArea` as a new section - New TypeScript types: `WebhookLog`, `WebhookLogSummary`, `WebhookLogListResponse` - New API service methods: `getWebhookLog`, `getWebhookLogSummary`, `retryWebhookEvent`, `saveHubSpotConfigWithSecret` **Infrastructure / Windows fixes** - Celery worker uses `--pool=threads` on Windows (prefork pool doesn't work on Windows) - Flask app context pushed via `worker_init` signal so tasks don't call `create_app()` in threads - `tech.md` steering updated: Docker references removed, WSL Redis and Celery threads commands added ### Tests 80+ tests across 8 new test files covering signature verification, deduplication, loop guard, webhook controller, log API endpoints, client secret encryption, cleanup job, and end-to-end integration. ### Setup Required 1. Add `HUBSPOT_CLIENT_SECRET=` to `backend/.env` 2. Configure webhook subscriptions in HubSpot (Settings → Private Apps → Webhooks tab) 3. Start Celery worker: `celery -A celery_worker.celery worker --loglevel=info --pool=threads --concurrency=4` 4. Start Celery Beat: `celery -A celery_worker.celery beat --loglevel=info` 5. Start Redis: `wsl -d Ubuntu -- redis-server --daemonize yes` --- ## Summary by cubic Adds real-time one-way HubSpot sync via webhooks with a simple UI to configure and monitor it. Also fixes base64 HMAC signatures, merges the beat schedule correctly, fixes retry handling, guards the stale-sync warning, and allows client‑secret‑only config updates. - **New Features** - `POST /api/hubspot/webhook` with HMAC‑SHA256 verification (v3: ms timestamps, base64 signatures). - `Celery` pipeline: process → fetch/upsert → match → convert → extract → rescore. - Dedup within 60s and a 30s loop guard to prevent write-back loops. - Webhook log APIs: `GET /webhook-log`, `GET /webhook-log/summary`, `POST /webhook-log/{id}/retry`. - Client secret storage with encryption; `POST /config` accepts `client_secret`; `GET /config` returns `has_client_secret`; auto-load from `HUBSPOT_CLIENT_SECRET`. - Hourly engagement sync task; interval via `HUBSPOT_ENGAGEMENT_SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES`. - Webhook Sync Panel in the UI: client secret setup, copyable webhook URL, setup guide, stale sync warning, 24‑hour summary, paginated log with Retry. - Tests tightened: assert retry task scheduling and use a consistent webhook URL in controller tests. - **Migration** - Run DB migrations. - Set `HUBSPOT_CLIENT_SECRET` (and optionally `HUBSPOT_ENGAGEMENT_SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES`). - In HubSpot, point webhook subscriptions to `/api/hubspot/webhook`. - Start Redis, `Celery` worker (`--pool=threads`) and `Celery Beat`. Written for commit 0b970c25f2df0090383fda9a365cdee95c724019. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * HubSpot webhook sync: unauthenticated receiver, signature verification, async processing, webhook logs, retries, loop-guard/deduplication, scheduled purge and engagement sync, frontend Webhook Sync panel and API support, and client-secret save/display (write-only). * **Documentation** * New end-to-end design, requirements, and task specs for HubSpot webhook sync; updated async Redis/Celery setup guidance. * **Tests** * Extensive unit & integration tests covering signature verification, webhook receiver, processing pipeline, cleanup, and config encryption. * **Chores** * DB migration and example env vars added for HubSpot settings. title: Feature/unified task list
body: # Unify HubSpot and native tasks in Lead Command Center ## Problem Leads were appearing in the Today's Action queue due to overdue HubSpot tasks, but when users opened the Lead Command Center the Open Tasks section showed "No open tasks." This created a confusing and broken experience — the queue banner referenced a task the user couldn't see or act on. Root cause: two separate task systems existed in parallel: - `lead_tasks` table — native CRM tasks created in the platform (shown in Command Center) - `tasks` table — HubSpot-imported tasks linked via `task_associations` (used by queue queries, but never shown in Command Center) ## Changes ### Backend - **`command_center_controller.py`** — `GET /api/leads/:id/command-center` now fetches open/overdue tasks from the `tasks` table (HubSpot-imported) alongside native `lead_tasks`, returning them in `open_tasks` with `source: 'hubspot'` and a prefixed ID (`hs-{id}`) - **`command_center_controller.py`** — New `POST /api/leads/:id/hubspot-tasks/:task_id/done` endpoint marks a HubSpot task as completed locally (does not sync to HubSpot), appends a `task_completed` timeline entry, and triggers RA recomputation so the lead can leave Today's Action queue ### Frontend - **`LeadTaskList.tsx`** — Filter now includes `status === 'overdue'` alongside `status === 'open'` so HubSpot tasks with `overdue` status are no longer dropped before rendering - **`LeadTaskList.tsx`** — HubSpot tasks render with an orange "HubSpot" chip and a "Mark Done" checkmark button (with tooltip: "Mark as done locally — does not update HubSpot"). Native tasks work exactly as before - **`api.ts`** — Added `callLogService.markHubSpotTaskDone(leadId, taskId)` - **`types/index.ts`** — Added `source?: 'native' | 'hubspot'` and `'overdue'` to `LeadTask`; expanded `CommandCenterPayload` with all fields accessed in the component ### Code quality guards (prevent missing-import regressions) - **`.eslintrc.cjs`** — Added `react/jsx-no-undef: error` to catch JSX components used without being imported - **`.git/hooks/pre-commit`** — Added `tsc --noEmit` (TypeScript type-check) and `npm run build` (full Vite production build) to the pre-commit hook alongside the existing migration chain check - Fixed all pre-existing TypeScript errors surfaced by enabling the checks (unused imports, unused variables, missing types across 15+ files) ## Testing - Verified against Manuel Medellin (lead_id=634): "Follow up on 3046 N Hamlin Ave" now appears in Open Tasks with the HubSpot badge and Mark Done button - `tsc --noEmit` exits 0 - `npm run build` succeeds (1976 modules, 7s) - Pre-commit hook runs all checks automatically on every commit --- ## Summary by cubic Unifies HubSpot-imported tasks with native tasks in the Lead Command Center so users can see and clear all open/overdue tasks. Adds a local “Mark Done” for HubSpot tasks to fix the Today’s Action mismatch. - **New Features** - Backend: `GET /api/leads/:id/command-center` now returns HubSpot tasks from `tasks` with `source='hubspot_import'` and status `open`/`overdue`, tagged with `source: 'hubspot'` and `id` prefixed `hs-`. Added `POST /api/leads/:id/hubspot-tasks/:task_id/done` with an atomic `UPDATE ... RETURNING` to mark a HubSpot task completed locally, append a `task_completed` timeline entry, and recompute RA (no HubSpot sync). - Frontend: `LeadTaskList` shows `overdue` tasks, adds a HubSpot chip and a “Mark Done” button with a tooltip (“does not update HubSpot”), and calls `callLogService.markHubSpotTaskDone`. `LeadCommandCenter` only completes native tasks (numeric IDs) and invalidates after HubSpot tasks are marked done. Types updated: `LeadTask.id` supports `number | string`, `LeadTaskStatus` includes `overdue`, `source` added; `CommandCenterPayload` expanded with fields used by the UI. - **Refactors** - Added ESLint `react/jsx-no-undef` and pre-commit checks (`tsc --noEmit`, `npm run build`); fixed TS errors. Cleanups: removed unused imports and unused page state in queues. Written for commit d830d2dfa1540a6c666603fc059ad5f6667938bc. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * HubSpot tasks can now be marked as complete directly from the command center alongside native tasks. * HubSpot tasks appear with a visual indicator (chip) to distinguish them from native tasks. * **Bug Fixes** * Fixed undefined navigation reference when clicking action buttons. * **Improvements** * Extended task status handling to support "overdue" state. * Enhanced data type flexibility for improved data completeness tracking. title: Fix/startup blockers
body: # Fix startup blockers: migration chain branches + duplicate mapsLoaded ## Problem The app on `main` would not start due to two independent issues: 1. **Branched Alembic migration chain** — Three pairs of migration files shared the same revision ID (`d4e5f6g7h8i9`, `f6g7h8i9j0k1`, `g7h8i9j0k1l2`), causing Alembic to detect multiple heads and the Flask startup check to abort the server. 2. **Duplicate `mapsLoaded` variable in `App.tsx`** — The same `useLoadScript` hook was declared twice in the same component scope, preventing the Vite build from completing. ## Changes ### Migration chain fix - Renamed duplicate revision IDs to unique suffixes (`d4e5f6g7h8i9b`, `e5f6g7h8i9j0b`, `f6g7h8i9j0k1b`, `g7h8i9j0k1l2b`) - Created two new merge migrations to linearize the chain: - `f6g7h8i9j0k1c_merge_confidence_and_rentcast.py` - `g7h8i9j0k1l2c_merge_sale_comp_and_socrata.py` - Updated downstream `down_revision` references to point to the new merge heads - Verified: `flask db heads` now returns a single head (`q7r8s9t0u1v2`) ### Frontend fix - Removed the duplicate `useLoadScript` / `mapsLoaded` declaration in `App.tsx` ### Guards to prevent recurrence (5 total) 1. **`backend/tests/test_migration_heads.py`** — Two pytest tests: one asserts a single Alembic head, one asserts all revision IDs are unique. Runs in CI with the rest of the test suite. 2. **`.git/hooks/pre-commit`** — Shell hook that blocks any commit introducing duplicate revision IDs or multiple heads. Runs automatically on every `git commit`. 3. **`python dev.py check`** — New standalone pre-flight command that checks revision uniqueness, single head, and `node_modules` presence. Also runs automatically inside `python dev.py` before Flask starts. 4. **`backend/run.py` linter** — Scans migration files at server startup (before `create_app()`) and raises `SystemExit` with the exact filenames if any revision ID is duplicated. ## Testing - `pytest tests/test_migration_heads.py` — 2 passed - `python dev.py check` — all checks passed - Backend starts cleanly on `http://localhost:5000` - Frontend builds and serves on `http://localhost:3000` --- ## Summary by cubic Fixes backend and frontend startup by linearizing Alembic migrations and removing a duplicate Google Maps loader. Hardens pre-flight checks so the app fails fast with clear errors. - **Bug Fixes** - Linearized Alembic chain: renamed duplicate revision IDs, added merge migrations (`f6g7h8i9j0k1c_merge_confidence_and_rentcast.py`, `g7h8i9j0k1l2c_merge_sale_comp_and_socrata.py`), updated `down_revision`; now a single head. - Removed duplicate `useLoadScript` (`mapsLoaded`) in `App.tsx` from `@react-google-maps/api` to restore the Vite build. - **New Features** - Hardened startup linter in `backend/run.py`: safely scans migrations and fails fast on duplicate revision IDs with clear guidance. - `python dev.py check` now verifies revision uniqueness, single head, that the DB is at head, and `frontend/node_modules`; exits non‑zero to block startup. - `backend/tests/test_migration_heads.py` enforces one Alembic head and unique revisions in CI. Written for commit 093f7189ff53b1a3cb2fa7397d136ec79bcc9c7b. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Chores** * Added database schema migrations with new columns and tables to support expanded backend functionality. * Enhanced system startup validation to detect and prevent migration configuration errors. * Added automated tests to ensure database migration integrity. * Improved API key handling in frontend configuration. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/11?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: feat: Actionable Lead Command Center — full implementation with queue…
body: # Actionable Lead Command Center ## Summary Implements a full CRM-style lead management system on top of the existing HubSpot import pipeline. Adds 7 work queues, a rich lead detail view, action engine, task management, call/note logging, and a complete activity timeline. ## What's New ### Backend **New models** - `LeadTask` — CRM-native tasks with typed task types and direct `lead_id` FK - `LeadTimelineEntry` — chronological activity log per lead - `LeadCRMFlagsView` — PostgreSQL view computing `has_phone`, `has_email`, `has_property_match` from source-of-truth tables (eliminates stale denormalized flags) **New services** - `ActionEngineService` — deterministic rule engine that classifies leads into 10 recommended actions based on data completeness, HubSpot signals, and task state - `QueueService` — badge counts and paginated rows for all 7 work queues; queries both `lead_tasks` and HubSpot `tasks` via `task_associations` - `LeadTaskService`, `LeadTimelineService`, `CallLogService` — task lifecycle, timeline pagination, call/note logging - `HubSpotTimelineImportService` — derives `is_warm` from HubSpot call records **New controllers** - `command_center_controller` — full lead detail endpoint with merged phones/emails from flat columns + relational tables, HubSpot interactions injected into timeline, marketing list membership, all lead fields - `queue_controller` — 7 queue endpoints + badge counts - `bulk_action_controller` — bulk suppress/DNC/status-change **New migrations** - `m3n4o5p6q7r8` — CRM columns on leads (lead_status, recommended_action, has_phone, has_email, has_property_match, etc.) with inline data backfill - `n4o5p6q7r8s9` — lead_tasks table - `o5p6q7r8s9t0` — lead_timeline_entries table - `p6q7r8s9t0u1` — lead_id + task_type on tasks table (unifies task tables) - `q7r8s9t0u1v2` — lead_crm_flags PostgreSQL view **Infrastructure improvements** - `dev.py` — pre-flight migration check blocks startup if DB is behind head - `app/__init__.py` — loud startup failure if migration fails + DB is not at head; CRM field backfill thread on first boot - `app/controllers/routes.py` — rich `/api/health` endpoint checking DB connectivity, migration head, Action Engine state, and queue counts - `.kiro/steering/migrations.md` — migration conventions doc enforcing idempotent `IF NOT EXISTS` SQL ### Frontend **New components** - `TodaysActionQueue`, `PreviouslyWarmQueue`, `FollowUpOverdueQueue`, `NoNextActionQueue`, `NeedsReviewQueue`, `DoNotContactQueue`, `MissingPropertyMatchQueue` — 7 queue views - `QueueTable` — reusable sortable table with clickable rows navigating to lead detail, built-in View button, Next Action column - `LeadCommandCenter` — two-column lead detail view with: - Back button + queue context banners (shows which queue(s) the lead is in and exactly why) - HubSpot deal stage chip + Status Note box prominently in header - Recommended Action panel, open tasks, Log Note/Call forms - Full activity timeline with HubSpot calls/notes and mailer history injected inline - Sticky sidebar with all lead data: phones (click-to-call), emails (click-to-copy), property details, mailing address, skip trace status, mailer history, marketing list membership, source metadata - `RecommendedActionPanel`, `LeadTaskList`, `LeadTimeline`, `LogNoteForm`, `LogCallForm` - `QueueSidebar` — live badge counts in left nav Work Queues section **Navigation** - Left nav "Work Queues" group with live badge counts polling every 60s - `/queues/*` routes for all 7 queues; `/leads/:id/command-center` for lead detail - Old `/leads/views/*` URLs redirect to new `/queues/*` ### Data backfills (run against local DB) - `has_phone`, `has_email` backfilled from flat phone/email columns + contact_phones/contact_emails - `has_property_match` backfilled from confirmed hubspot_matches - `hubspot_deal_stage` backfilled from `hubspot_deals.raw_payload.properties.dealstage` with human-readable labels (Decision Maker Bought In, Deprioritize, Awaiting Skip Trace, Contract Sent, etc.) - Action Engine bulk recompute run on all 6,789 leads ## Testing - 93 backend tests passing (queue service, queue controller, queue integration, action engine, command center controller, call log service, lead task service, lead timeline service) - TypeScript: no errors in changed files ## How to test 1. Open http://localhost:3000 2. Navigate to Properties → Work Queues → Today's Action 3. Click any lead row to open the Lead Command Center 4. Verify: back button, queue context banner, HubSpot stage chip, status note, phones/emails with copy buttons, timeline with HubSpot call history --- ## Summary by cubic Implements the Actionable Lead Command Center: seven work queues with live counts and a lead detail view showing recommended actions, tasks, call/note logging, and a unified timeline. Adds a deterministic Action Engine, CRM tasks, and APIs with a React UI; now with fail‑fast migration checks, idempotent migrations, improved error handling, and a LeadTimeline pagination reset fix. - **New Features** - Seven queues with counts and APIs, plus React views: Today’s Action, Previously Warm, Follow‑Up Overdue, No Next Action, Needs Review, Do Not Contact, Missing Property Match. - Lead Command Center page: recommended action panel, open tasks, log note/call forms, unified timeline (includes HubSpot imports), and a data‑rich sidebar. - Action Engine computes 10 recommended actions with single/bulk Celery recompute tasks. - CRM task model and lifecycle with create/complete/snooze; bulk suppress/DNC/status endpoints. - Health check expanded: DB connectivity, migration head, unclassified lead count, and queue count probes. - New backend primitives: timeline entries, computed flags view; queue service for badge counts and paginated rows. - **Bug Fixes** - Idempotent Alembic migrations with `IF NOT EXISTS`, plus fail‑fast startup if the DB is behind head (also enforced in `dev.py`). - Consistent controller error handling and new typed errors (e.g., `LeadTaskValidationError`, invalid status transitions); schema cleanup and stricter validation. - Legacy routes now use 308 redirects to preserve HTTP methods (e.g., `/leads/views/*`, analyze, scoring weights). - Frontend stability: guards for null/unknown states and improved form validation; LeadTimeline now resets to page 1 when the lead changes to avoid missing entries; fixed a NameError in queue integration tests. Written for commit 0f1d0426ff1daf6133beb09aa81017529d3b1622. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes **New Features** - Actionable Lead Command Center dashboard with comprehensive lead details view - Seven organized queue views (Today's Action, Previously Warm, Follow-Up Overdue, No Next Action, Needs Review, Do Not Contact, Missing Property Match) with badge counts in navigation - Lead task management—create, complete, and snooze tasks directly from lead details - Activity timeline tracking with call and note logging capabilities - Lead status lifecycle management (Active, Nurture, Do Not Contact, Suppressed) - Property match indicator and research tracking - Recommended action engine guiding user next steps [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/10?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: feat: HubSpot CRM migration, property-contact model, and work queue fixes
body: ## HubSpot CRM Migration, Property-Contact Model & Work Queue Fixes ### Summary This PR delivers the full HubSpot CRM migration feature, the property-contact data model, and a series of reliability fixes that make all 6 work queue views operational with real data. --- ### What's Included #### HubSpot CRM Migration - Full import pipeline for deals, contacts, companies, and engagements via paginated Celery tasks with upsert (no duplicates on re-import) - Matcher service: PIN match → address match → UNMATCHED, with confidence levels HIGH/MEDIUM/UNMATCHED - Activity converter: HubSpot notes/calls → Interactions, HubSpot tasks → Tasks, with lead association resolution - Signal extractor: keyword-based extraction of 12 signal types (PRIOR_WARM_CONVERSATION, DO_NOT_CONTACT, etc.) from engagement body text - Lead scoring integration: signal adjustments (+20 for APPOINTMENT_OCCURRED, -50 for DO_NOT_CONTACT, etc.) - Review Queue UI: filterable table with side-by-side comparison, confirm/reject/re-link/mark-new actions - Timeline panel, note/task creation forms, organization model with audit log - 6 work queue views: Previously Warm, Needs Review, Follow-Up Overdue, No Next Action, Do Not Contact, Missing Property Match #### Property-Contact Model - Contact, ContactPhone, ContactEmail, PropertyContact models replacing flat owner/phone/email columns - ContactService, property_controller (renamed from lead_controller with backward-compat redirect), contact_controller - ContactsSection and ContactFormModal frontend components #### Pipeline Reliability & Startup Recovery - Background thread runs full pipeline (match → convert → extract signals → rescore) after every import, regardless of Celery/Redis availability - Startup recovery detects and fixes: orphaned interactions (no lead associations), orphaned tasks (no task associations), missing signals, stale overdue task statuses - PostgreSQL connection pool capped at 3 per process; Celery workers skip auto-migrate to prevent connection exhaustion - `run.py`: `use_reloader=False` fixes WinError 10038 crash on Windows #### Work Queue Fixes - Follow-Up Overdue: query now catches `status='open' AND due_date < NOW()` in addition to `status='overdue'`; startup bulk-marks past-due tasks as overdue - All 6 queues verified with real data: Previously Warm (164), Follow-Up Overdue (257), No Next Action (822), Do Not Contact (337), Needs Review (0 — correct), Missing Property Match (0 — correct) #### Schema Contract & Testing - Zod `.safeParse()` with console error logging replaces `.parse()` on all lead view endpoints — schema mismatches no longer surface as "Network error" - `mailer_history` Zod schema and TypeScript type updated to accept `string | array | object | null` (legacy spreadsheet import data) - `backend/tests/test_api_contract.py`: 10 contract tests covering all 6 view endpoints and 3 `mailer_history` type variants #### Celery Beat Schedules - Nightly: `hubspot.extract_signals`, `hubspot.rescore_leads` - Hourly: `tasks.mark_overdue` (keeps overdue status current) --- ### Testing - All 10 new contract tests pass - 21 multifamily property tests pass - All 6 work queues verified with live data against the production HubSpot import ### Notes - Temporary cleanup scripts (`check_db_counts.py`, `cleanup_placeholder_leads.py`, etc.) were excluded from this commit - The startup recovery threads are self-disabling once data is backfilled — they will not re-run on subsequent restarts once associations and signals exist --- ## Summary by cubic Migrates HubSpot CRM data into a property‑first CRM with import, matching, timeline, and scoring; adds a first‑class contact model and fixes all work queues. Tightens security with GET‑only HubSpot access and header‑based user identity. - **New Features** - HubSpot import for deals/contacts/companies/engagements via Celery with upsert; read‑only client with GET‑only enforcement; nightly signal extraction + rescore. - Match service (PIN → HIGH, address/email/name → MEDIUM/LOW/UNMATCHED) with a review queue and confirm/reject/re‑link actions. - Engagement conversion to Interactions/Tasks and unified timelines; overdue detection fixed and kept current hourly. - Lead scoring updated with extracted signals; new `suppression_flag` and `recommended_action` on properties. - Property‑contact model: `Contact`, `ContactPhone`, `ContactEmail`, `PropertyContact`; new contact APIs and UI (`ContactsSection`, `ContactFormModal`); case‑insensitive email index on `contact_emails` for faster matching (Alembic migration included). - Properties API replaces leads (legacy routes redirect); new organization model with audit log and link endpoints. - Reliability: post‑import background pipeline, startup recovery (orphans, signals, stale overdue), SQL pool cap. - OpenAPI at `/api/openapi.json`; safer schema handling; user ID read from `X-User-Id` header only. - **Migration** - Apply DB migrations, then set `HUBSPOT_ENCRYPTION_KEY` in backend `.env`. - Run Redis and a Celery worker (or `python dev.py` from project root to start Redis, Celery, and Flask together). - Ensure Celery Beat is running for nightly `hubspot.extract_signals` and hourly `tasks.mark_overdue`. - Update clients to use `/api/properties/*` and new contact/organization endpoints (legacy `/api/leads/*` still works). - Optional: regenerate frontend types from `/api/openapi.json`. - Repo hygiene: Celery Beat schedule files are ignored via `.gitignore` (no action needed). Written for commit d5ace668d0701e381fbabc5932666d896ac395a2. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Added HubSpot CRM integration with import, matching, and review workflows * Introduced Contact management system with primary contact designation per property * Added unified timeline view for interactions and tasks * New Organization management with audit logging * Enhanced property filtering by completion status and signal-based views * Support for suppression flags and recommended actions on properties * **Documentation** * Updated setup and development instructions with new environment configuration [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/9?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

title: feat: AI fetch comps timeout fix, async job pattern, ESLint setup, us…
body:
# feat: AI fetch comps reliability, async job pattern, ESLint + useAIMutation hook

## Summary

Fixes a silent timeout bug where Fetch Comps would fail after 30s with no user feedback, adds an async Celery-backed job pattern for long-running AI calls, and introduces ESLint with a custom rule + a shared `useAIMutation` hook to prevent the class of race condition bugs that caused the original issue.

## Changes

### Bug Fix — Axios timeout for AI fetch endpoints
- `frontend/src/services/api.ts`: `fetchRentCompsAI` and `fetchSaleCompsAI` now pass `{ timeout: 120_000 }` overriding the global 30s default. Gemini with web search grounding can take 30–90s; the previous 30s timeout caused silent failures.

### Backend — Async job pattern (Option 2 infrastructure)
- `backend/celery_worker.py`: Added `multifamily.fetch_rent_comps_ai` and `multifamily.fetch_sale_comps_ai` Celery tasks with `time_limit=180` / `soft_time_limit=160`. Added both to `REQUIRED_TASKS` startup assertion.
- `backend/app/controllers/multifamily_market_rent_controller.py`: `POST /fetch-ai` now accepts `?async=true` to enqueue a Celery task and return `{ job_id, status: "pending" }` (HTTP 202). Added `GET /fetch-ai/status/:job_id` polling endpoint.
- `backend/app/controllers/multifamily_sale_comp_controller.py`: Same async pattern for sale comps.
- `frontend/src/services/api.ts`: Added `getRentCompsAIJobStatus` and `getSaleCompsAIJobStatus` methods.

### Frontend — `useAIMutation` hook
- `frontend/src/hooks/useAIMutation.ts`: New shared hook encapsulating mutation, cycling progress labels, and inline status state. Button disabled/loading state is driven directly from `mutation.isPending` — the anti-pattern (mirroring `isPending` into `useState`) is structurally impossible when using this hook.
- `frontend/src/components/multifamily/SaleCompsTab.tsx`: Refactored to use `useAIMutation`.
- `frontend/src/components/multifamily/MarketRentsTab.tsx`: Refactored to use `useAIMutation`.

### ESLint setup
- `frontend/.eslintrc.cjs`: Full ESLint config with `@typescript-eslint`, `eslint-plugin-react`, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`.
- `frontend/eslint-local-rules/index.cjs`: Custom `no-mirror-query-state` rule — errors when a `useEffect` calls a `useState` setter conditioned on `isPending`, `isLoading`, `isFetching`, `isError`, or `isSuccess`. Catches the exact race condition pattern at lint time.
- `frontend/package.json`: Added `lint` and `lint:strict` scripts.

### Inline messaging (Task 11 completion)
- `frontend/src/context/NotificationContext.tsx`: Global sticky error banner replacing Snackbar.
- `frontend/src/main.tsx`: `MutationCache.onError` global handler wired to `globalNotify`.
- Both tabs use inline `Alert` + `Collapse` instead of Snackbar for fetch results.

## What was tested
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — exit code 0, no errors
- ESLint run — zero errors/warnings in new files; 10 pre-existing errors in untouched files
- TypeScript diagnostics clean on all new/modified files

## Notes
- The async path (`?async=true`) is infrastructure-only — the frontend currently uses the synchronous path with the 120s timeout. The polling endpoints are ready if needed.
- Pre-existing ESLint errors in `MarketingListManager.tsx`, `PropertyFactsForm.tsx`, `ScoreLegend.tsx`, `AnalysisLandingPage.tsx`, `OMUnitMixComparison.tsx` (unescaped JSX entities, extra semicolon) are not addressed in this PR.

## Summary by cubic
Builds a full Commercial OM PDF intake pipeline with async processing, a review UI, and a confirm-to-deal flow. Also improves comps reliability, caching, migrations, rate limits, and centralizes user identity via `X-User-Id`.

- **New Features**
  - Commercial OM PDF intake: upload → async pipeline (parse → extract → research) → review → confirm creates a Deal; status polling and data warnings.
  - Async comps: optional `?async=true` jobs for rent/sale comps with status endpoints; Celery hard time limits; endpoints rate‑limited to 10/hour.
  - Frontend: OM upload/status/review pages; editable unit mix with instant recalcs via a pure scenario engine; global banner + `useAIMutation` for long‑running actions.
  - Developer tooling: ESLint with `@typescript-eslint`, `eslint-plugin-react`, and local `no-mirror-query-state`; smoke/contract tests and a one‑command dev launcher.
  - Address UX: Google Places autocomplete via `@react-google-maps/api`.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Comps fetches: increase timeout to 120s; non‑overlapping status polling with error backoff.
  - RentCast cache: deterministic 90‑day key now includes `property_type`; skip caching fallback responses; strip tzinfo before comparing to naive DB datetimes; defensive parsing.
  - Database/Contracts: merged heads; sale comp `cap_rate` nullable with `noi` and `cap_rate_confidence`; OM confirm wrapped in DB savepoints; rehab/rent‑roll responses use snake_case keys; centralized user identity via `X-User-Id`; request schemas ignore unknown fields.
  - Rate limiting: removed global defaults; apply per‑route limits to protect expensive endpoints.
  - Migrations: fail‑fast downgrades, `IF EXISTS` constraint drops, SQLite‑safe constraint removals, and broadened “constraint not found” handling to catch `OperationalError`, `InternalError`, and `ProgrammingError` (SQLSTATE 42704).

title: fix: ARV analysis flow — reliability, UX, and error handling
body: See PR description below — opening for review. --- ## Summary by cubic Stabilizes the 6‑step ARV analysis with a more reliable comparable search, cleaner UX, and clearer errors. Adds Gemini‑powered comps, an optional async flow (sync by default), a local Socrata cache, confidence scoring, and stronger tests. - **New Features** - Gemini comparable search with structured comps + narrative (new `GeminiNarrativePanel`, `similarityNotes` in review). Uses gemini‑2.5‑flash with expanded output. - Optional async Step 2 via Celery (sync by default). Auto‑advance to review; safe sync fallback if enqueue fails. Added `docker-compose.yml`, `Procfile`, `start.ps1`. - Local PostgreSQL cache of Cook County datasets with refresh task and APIs: `GET /api/cache/socrata/status`, `POST /api/cache/socrata/sync`. - Workflow UX: progress stepper, URL‑driven session state via React Query, Google Places autocomplete via `@react-google-maps/api` + `use-places-autocomplete`, accepts Places lat/lng; allows manual entry if Maps isn’t ready. - Valuation confidence score and adaptive ARV range; `min_comparables` added to scoring weights. Migrations/env updates; deps include `honcho` and `fast-check`. - **Bug Fixes** - Comparable search: fixed boolean SQL comparisons, extended lookback to 36 months, improved radius/caching, guarded Gemini JSON parsing for non‑dict responses. - Async flow: enforce sequential steps, ensure `completed_steps` appends `COMPARABLE_SEARCH`, advance to `COMPARABLE_REVIEW` on completion, rollback before writing error state, reset `session.loading` on enqueue failure. - Error handling: Axios interceptor surfaces backend `error.message`; controllers re‑raise domain exceptions; 500s redact raw exceptions; safer Redis URL redaction. - Schema/migrations: aligned enum values with DB, idempotent Alembic across PostgreSQL/SQLite, correct cache table/column definitions, cascade fixes on `ValuationResult`, added `completed_steps`, `step_results`, and `loading` columns. - Cache/status: robust stale‑days parsing, null‑safe NOT NULL mappings, stable status reporting when rows exist without a successful sync. Written for commit 825d3f5ec87a7cca24a520f6e75e7a4cba317fae. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes **New Features** * Comparable search now powered by AI for better property matching * Async comparable search with real-time polling status (no more timeouts) * Google Places autocomplete for address input with coordinate fallback * AI-generated narrative explaining comparable selections * Confidence scoring displayed for valuations * Similarity notes column in comparable review table * Local data caching for improved performance **Bug Fixes** * Extended comparable search date lookback to capture more recent sales * Fixed timeout errors during comparable search step **Improvements** * Better handling of property type and condition enums * Enhanced coordinate resolution from multiple sources * ARV range adapts based on valuation confidence [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/bendstaples7/B-B-Commercial-RE-Underwriting/pull/7?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)
